body_paragraphs: Two threads that should move together didn't yesterday. Stocks rallied hard - the S&P and Nasdaq both snapped three-day losing streaks on chip earnings - even as Brent crude pushed past $91 and Fed hike odds ticked up to 16.6% from 13.3%. Markets are pricing risk and opportunity separately this week, which rarely lasts long. Mediators are still pushing a 10-day ceasefire in the Iran conflict; Washington is simultaneously moving fighter jets into the region in case it fails.

Locally, your two summer storylines diverged further. The Marlins' skid reached eight straight, their longest since April 2024, with Max Meyer's neck strain now landing him on the IL for a fifth consecutive season. The Dolphins, meanwhile, quietly locked up linebacker Jordyn Brooks on a three-year extension, their third rebuild cornerstone signed this offseason. One team's floor is falling out; the other's foundation is getting poured in real time.

Beyond the feed: Cecilia Vicuna's thirty-foot red wool sculpture at PAMM, built around the ancient Andean quipu, closes August 9 and is worth seeing before it comes down. In Hong Kong, researchers built a plastic that eats itself in six days flat, using bacteria that wake up on command. Neither will matter next week. Both are the kind of thing worth actually noticing this one.

read_only_three.label: Stocks rally while hike odds and oil both climb
The skid hits eight - longest since 2024
A 30-foot quipu closes soon at PAMM

what_changed.title: Marlins' skid reaches eight straight
what_changed.previous_state: Entering Tuesday, Miami had lost seven straight, including Monday's loss to Houston.
what_changed.current_state: Miami lost again Tuesday, 5-8, its eighth consecutive loss - the franchise's longest skid since April 2024.
what_changed.why_it_matters: The June cushion is now fully gone, and the schedule isn't getting easier.

what_changed.title: Dolphins lock up Jordyn Brooks through 2029
what_changed.previous_state: Camp opened Monday with rookies reporting; no financial commitments had been made public.
what_changed.current_state: Miami signed Brooks to a three-year, $51.3M extension Tuesday - the rebuild's third pillar signing after Achane and Brewer.
what_changed.why_it_matters: The first real financial signal of how committed this rebuild is to its own timeline.

what_changed.title: Stocks snap a three-day losing streak
what_changed.previous_state: Monday's close was down slightly (-0.19% S&P), pressured by oil.
what_changed.current_state: The S&P rose 0.89% and the Nasdaq 1.29% Tuesday, led by a chip-earnings rally.
what_changed.why_it_matters: Equities and the Fed's rate math are now telling two different stories at once.

what_changed.title: Hike odds tick back up to 16.6%
what_changed.previous_state: Hike odds were 13.3% as of Monday's CME FedWatch reading.
what_changed.current_state: Hike odds rose to 16.6% (83.4% hold) as of Tuesday, even as June CPI data (released July 14) showed inflation cooling to 3.5%.
what_changed.why_it_matters: The Fed's caution is now visibly about oil and Iran, not the inflation print itself.

what_changed.title: Brent tops $91 as a new ceasefire proposal emerges
what_changed.previous_state: Brent was at $89.22 Monday, up about 20% this month.
what_changed.current_state: Brent rose another 2.11% to $91.10 Tuesday, even as Qatar, Egypt, and Pakistan proposed a 10-day ceasefire.
what_changed.why_it_matters: The market isn't yet pricing in the ceasefire proposal succeeding.

themes.title: Ten-day ceasefire, or ten more days of strikes
themes.thesis: Mediators want a pause; Washington is preparing for the opposite.
themes.synthesis: Qatar, Egypt, and Pakistan have proposed a 10-day ceasefire meant to revive last month's collapsed interim deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but the U.S. carried out fresh strikes Monday night as the Houthis threatened their own blockade of Saudi Arabia. Iran now describes itself as in full-scale war with the U.S., and the Pentagon has moved fighter jets and refueling aircraft into the region - preparation, officials say, for the talks to fail. Defense Secretary Hegseth put the war's cost at $37.5 billion as of Tuesday.
themes.new_today: The 10-day ceasefire proposal is new; so is the $37.5B cost estimate and Iran's full-scale war framing.
themes.why_it_matters: Every day this runs past the FOMC meeting is another day oil is doing the Fed's job for it - just not in a way anyone would choose.

sources.headline: U.S. strikes Iran and Houthis threaten Saudi Arabia shipping as mediators push 10-day ceasefire
Iran mediators push new ceasefire as Trump eyes all-out war

themes.title: Stocks rally, hike odds rise - both at once
themes.thesis: The market did two contradictory things in the same session.
themes.synthesis: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq both snapped three-day losing streaks Tuesday, up 0.89% and 1.29% on a chip-led earnings rally led by Micron. At the same time, CME FedWatch odds of a July 29 hike ticked up to 16.6% (from 13.3% Monday) as Brent crude pushed past $91. June's CPI print, released July 14, actually showed headline inflation cooling to 3.5% y/y (core 2.6%) - better than expected - which is why the Fed's caution now reads as almost entirely about oil and geopolitics, not the underlying data.
themes.new_today: Stocks rallied and hike odds rose in the same session - a genuine divergence, not noise.
themes.why_it_matters: This is the actual input to CRE debt costs and cap rates for the back half of the year: not whether inflation is cooling, which it is, but whether oil forces the Fed's hand anyway.

sources.headline: Stock Market Today (July 21, 2026): Nasdaq, S&P 500 climbs as earnings lift market, Middle East tensions loom

themes.title: Eight straight, and it's not just bad luck anymore
themes.thesis: This is now the longest losing streak in two years, not a rough week.
themes.synthesis: Miami fell 5-8 to Houston again Tuesday, its eighth consecutive loss - the franchise's longest skid since April 2024. Max Meyer, the All-Star right-hander, landed on the 15-day IL with a neck strain; it's the fifth straight season he's made an IL trip. The streak has erased essentially all of the cushion built during a franchise-record 20-6 June, and it started before the All-Star break even ended.
themes.new_today: The streak extended to eight Tuesday night; Meyer's IL history is now a recognized pattern, not a one-off.
themes.why_it_matters: The wild-card math is unforgiving in a division this tight - eight losses in three weeks is the difference between a real October push and a September fade.

sources.headline: Losing Streak Hits Seven as Astros Take Care of Marlins Monday
Marlins' Losing Streak Reaches Seven With Loss to Astros
Miami Marlins 5 - Houston Astros 8: Final score, results, recap, box score, stats

themes.title: The rebuild just got its third anchor
themes.thesis: A patience-year roster doesn't lock up a linebacker through 2029 unless it means it.
themes.synthesis: Miami signed Jordyn Brooks to a three-year, $51.3 million extension ($35M guaranteed) Tuesday, keeping him through 2029. GM Jon-Eric Sullivan and coach Jeff Hafley identified Brooks early as one of the rebuild's pillars - he's now the third such player extended this offseason, joining running back De'Von Achane and center Aaron Brewer.
themes.new_today: The Brooks extension was announced Tuesday, the first hard financial commitment of camp week.
themes.why_it_matters: This is the tell for how serious the Hafley regime is about its timeline - you don't guarantee $35M to a linebacker in a true tank year.

sources.headline: Linebacker Jordyn Brooks agrees to 3-year extension with Dolphins, AP source says
Report: Dolphins LB Jordyn Brooks agrees to three-year, $51.3M extension
Jordyn Brooks signs multi-year contract extension with Miami Dolphins ahead of training camp

themes.title: An eight-story bet on a $1 billion neighborhood
themes.thesis: The stadium got the headlines; the civic center next door is the more interesting government story.
themes.synthesis: Miami topped off an eight-story, 257,000-square-foot Civic Center at Miami Freedom Park on July 10, days after securing a $193 million construction permit. The building - developed by Adler Development, built by Moss Construction - is part of the 130-acre, $1 billion Freedom Park project anchored by Inter Miami's stadium, which also includes a $20 million pedestrian bridge from the Metrorail station. Completion is targeted for spring 2028.
themes.new_today: First look at the topped-off structure and the project's full scope.
themes.why_it_matters: A real, financed, under-construction civic asset next to a private stadium deal - worth tracking as a model for how the city pairs public buildings with private development.

sources.headline: Miami secures $193M permit to build civic center at Miami Freedom Park

themes.title: The summer list is almost fully checked off
themes.thesis: The anticipated list from three weeks ago is nearly exhausted - which means it's time to look at fall's.
themes.synthesis: Manoli finally opened in Coconut Grove this week, alongside Crisppi's, Redhead Burger (Doral), Star Burger, and Flight West in South Miami. That leaves La Sponda and PopUp Bagels as the only holdovers from the original summer list still pending. Fall's list is already forming: Genghis Cohen, Royale Pizza Napoletana, Miami Slice, and Levain Bakery's first Miami location, opening in Coconut Grove.
themes.new_today: Manoli's opening closes out most of the original summer list; the fall list is now public.
themes.why_it_matters: Worth booking the summer stragglers before attention - and reservations - shift to the fall names.

sources.headline: New Miami restaurant openings and closings this week
The 6 most anticipated Miami restaurant openings this fall

themes.title: Rest is not the wellness problem it's marketed as
themes.thesis: The Stoics didn't treat rest as indulgence - they treated it as maintenance.
themes.synthesis: A cluster of recent Stoic writing converges on the same corrective: burnout isn't fixed by meditation apps or boundary-setting scripts, it's a signal that effort is going somewhere that doesn't compound. The Stoic frame treats deliberate rest, reflection, and reading as part of the discipline itself, not a break from it - what one recent piece called a resting ethic rather than a reward earned by output.
themes.new_today: Nothing urgent - this is standing practice, offered on a week that could use it.
themes.why_it_matters: A useful correction for anyone measuring a day's worth by how much of it got spent.

sources.headline: Finding Your Pace: Stoic Advice for Dealing with Burnout
How to Prevent & Overcome Burnout - by Stoic Philosophy

themes.title: Plastic that eats itself in six days
themes.thesis: The most useful materials science this month solves a problem by building in its own ending.
themes.synthesis: Researchers at the Chinese University of Hong Kong embedded dormant Bacillus subtilis spores into a common 3D-printing polymer. Left alone, the plastic behaves normally; heated, the spores wake up and secrete two cooperating enzymes - one that chops the polymer into pieces, one that chews those pieces into base molecules - breaking the material down completely in six days with no microplastic residue.
themes.new_today: Published mid-July; this is the first cycle of coverage.
themes.why_it_matters: A small, concrete example of solving a waste problem by design rather than by better recycling - worth noticing precisely because it isn't trying to be a headline.

sources.headline: New living plastic self-destructs in just 6 days without leaving microplastics
Bacteria-boosted living plastic breaks down on command

rabbit_hole.title: U.S. Real Estate Market Outlook 2026: Capital Markets
rabbit_hole.creator: CBRE Research
rabbit_hole.why: Ties together everything else in today's edition - the oil shock, the Fed's caution, and the actual cap-rate mechanics - into the one lens that matters most for your own work. Twenty-five minutes here is worth more than another day of headline-chasing on the Iran story.

beautiful_thing.title: Quipu Gut
beautiful_thing.creator: Cecilia Vicuna
beautiful_thing.body: A 30-foot column of dyed red, unspun wool - fifty strands, knotted rhythmically - hanging from the double-height ceiling of PAMM's main gallery. Vicuna, a Chilean poet and artist born in Santiago in 1948, built the piece around the quipu, the knotted-cord system Andean societies used to record information for centuries before European contact. It was first shown at documenta 14 in Kassel, Germany, alongside a video of Vicuna and her crew performing the ritual steps of the installation, chanting as they worked. It closes at PAMM on August 9.

noticing_prompt.text: Count how many times today you say yes to something out of habit before you've actually decided - the gap between the two is usually smaller than it feels.

evening_quote.text: Men are disturbed not by the things which happen, but by the opinions about the things.

evening_unwind.evening_prompt: What did you carry today because you decided to, and what did you carry only because putting it down felt like it wasn't allowed?

quote.text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.

threads.title: US-Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz
threads.current_state: Mediators (Qatar, Egypt, Pakistan) have proposed a 10-day ceasefire to revive last month's collapsed deal, but the U.S. struck again Monday night and the Houthis are threatening a Saudi blockade. Iran calls it a full-scale war; the Pentagon has moved fighter jets and refueling aircraft into the region. Brent is at $91.10, up 2.11% Tuesday. Hegseth estimates the war has cost $37.5B.

timeline.summary: First tracked: nine nights of US strikes, oil up about 20% this month, 10-day ceasefire being mediated.
10-day ceasefire formally proposed by Qatar/Egypt/Pakistan; US strikes continue, Iran calls it full-scale war, Brent tops $91, war cost estimated at $37.5B.

threads.title: Federal Reserve rate path
threads.current_state: FOMC meets July 28-29. Hike odds ticked up to 16.6% (83.4% hold) Tuesday from 13.3% Monday, even as June CPI (released July 14) showed inflation cooling to 3.5% y/y, core 2.6%. 10Y at 4.60%. Stocks rallied Tuesday (S&P +0.89%, Nasdaq +1.29%) on chip earnings despite the oil-driven Fed caution.

timeline.summary: Hike odds rising toward 46%; BofA sees three hikes by December.
Fed week arrives; positioning visible in Friday's chip-led selloff.
Hike odds collapse to 13.3% (86.7% hold) amid Iran-driven oil shock; 10Y ticks up to 4.59%.
Hike odds tick back up to 16.6% as oil keeps climbing; stocks rally anyway on chip earnings; June CPI confirmed at 3.5%.
